user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my recent personal records so that I can track strength milestones.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/prs/?limit=N requires JWT auth. limit defaults to 20, max 100. Returns list of PersonalRecordSerializer: {id, exercise_name, category, value, previous_value, metric, date, created_at}. Ordered by -date, -created_at. Cached 120s per-tenant. PRs are auto-detected (and created) by detect_prs() on every strength workout save (only for status=done and category=strength, using est_1rm).
inventory_notes: detect_prs creates a new PersonalRecord row whenever the computed est_1rm for any exercise exceeds the previous max for that exercise+metric. Only metric=est_1rm is detected automatically. detect_prs is also called by HealthKit sync (on matched_planned completion) and by runtime log/update paths.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create, update, and delete fitness goals so that I can track targets like a strength or endurance milestone.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/goals/ returns all FuelGoal rows for the tenant (no limit). POST creates a goal with exercise_name, metric, target_value, optional target_date and achieved_at. PATCH /api/v1/fuel/goals/{goal_id}/ partially updates. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/goals/{goal_id}/ removes. All require JWT auth. 404 on PATCH/DELETE if not found. FuelGoal fields: exercise_name, metric, target_value, target_date, achieved_at.
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:FuelGoalListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:FuelGoalDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/goals/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/goals/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/goals/{goal_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/goals/{goal_id}/
inventory_notes: No automatic goal-achievement detection — achieved_at must be set explicitly. Goals are not linked to PRs programmatically.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log and manage my daily body weight so that I can track weight trends over time.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/?limit=N returns up to N (default 90, max 365) entries ordered by -date. POST /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ upserts by (tenant, date): if a row for that date exists it is updated (returns 200), otherwise created (returns 201). Requires date (YYYY-MM-DD) and weight_kg. PATCH /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{entry_id}/ partially updates; returns 409 if moving to a date already occupied by another entry (date_conflict). DELETE /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{entry_id}/ returns 204. All require JWT auth.
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:BodyWeightListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:BodyWeightDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{entry_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{entry_id}/
inventory_notes: The model has a unique_together on (tenant, date). PATCH date-change conflict returns 409 with {error: date_conflict} instead of an opaque DB IntegrityError.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log and manage my daily resting heart rate so that I can track cardiovascular fitness trends.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/?limit=N returns up to N (default 90, max 365) entries ordered by -date. POST upserts by date: requires date (YYYY-MM-DD) and bpm (integer 20-250); validates bpm inline (400 if out of range). PATCH /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{entry_id}/ partially updates. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{entry_id}/ returns 204. All require JWT auth. unique_together=(tenant, date).
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:RestingHRListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:RestingHRDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{entry_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{entry_id}/
inventory_notes: Resting HR is also ingested via HealthKit sync's `daily_metrics[].resting_hr` field. The envelope section surfaces the latest entry within 3 days.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log and manage my daily sleep data so that I can see how rest correlates with my training.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/?limit=N returns up to N (default 90, max 365) entries ordered by -date. POST upserts by date: requires date (YYYY-MM-DD) and duration_hours (Decimal, 0-24); optional quality (1-5, silently ignored if invalid) and notes. Returns 201/200 based on created/updated. PATCH /api/v1/fuel/sleep/{entry_id}/ partially updates. DELETE returns 204. All require JWT auth. unique_together=(tenant, date).
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:SleepListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:SleepDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/sleep/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/sleep/{entry_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/sleep/{entry_id}/
inventory_notes: Sleep is also ingested via HealthKit sync's `daily_metrics[].sleep_hours`. User-entered quality/notes survive a HealthKit upsert because `update_or_create` only touches the keys in `defaults`.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create, browse, and manage reusable workout templates so that I can log frequent sessions quickly.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/templates/?category=<cat> returns all templates for the tenant, optionally filtered by category. POST creates a template (name, category, activity required; optional duration_minutes and detail_json). GET /api/v1/fuel/templates/{template_id}/ returns detail. PATCH partially updates. DELETE returns 204. All require JWT auth. Templates are ordered by -updated_at.
inventory_notes: Templates are separate from WorkoutPlan.schedule_json; they are user-created reusable blueprints, not plan-linked slots.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view, create, update, and delete workout plans from the web dashboard so that I can manage multi-week training programs.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/plans/?status=<status> lists up to 20 plans (newest first) with workout_count and completed_count. POST creates a plan and expands the schedule_json into individual planned Workout rows via _expand_plan_workouts (creates PlanSlot rows and links Workout.slot FK). PATCH /api/v1/fuel/plans/{plan_id}/ partially updates; if schedule_json or weeks change, deletes future planned workouts and re-expands remaining weeks from the regen_start date. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/plans/{plan_id}/ deletes planned workouts and orphans completed workouts (sets plan=null). All require JWT auth.
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:WorkoutPlanListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:WorkoutPlanDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/plans/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/plans/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/plans/{plan_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/plans/{plan_id}/
inventory_notes: Consumer PATCH regen uses old DELETE+re-expand, not the reconciler (which preserves UUIDs). The runtime PATCH uses the reconciler. No cron management in the consumer path.

feature: HealthKit Sync — Workout + Daily Metrics Ingestion
user_story: As an iOS subscriber, I want to sync my Apple Health workouts and daily health metrics so that my fitness data is automatically reflected in my assistant context.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/healthkit/sync/ requires JWT auth and rate-limited to 60/hour. Body: {workouts: [...], daily_metrics: [...], deleted_external_ids: [...], device_tz: 'IANA/zone'}. Gates: 404 if no tenant, 403 if suspended, 409 if fuel_disabled, 400 if body is not JSON/empty/exceeds caps (workouts<=50, daily_metrics<=31, deleted_external_ids<=100). Deletions are processed first (source=HEALTHKIT rows only; matched-planned rows with HK external_id survive because they have source=assistant/user). Tombstones are recorded on FuelProfile.healthkit_tombstones (FIFO-capped at 200) via the post_delete signal. Each workout is ingested atomically: idempotency on external_id; planned-auto-complete with select_for_update; existing manual DONE log adoption (merges HK metrics without duplicating). Daily metrics (resting_hr, sleep_hours, body_weight_kg) are upserted per-date; user quality/notes survive. Post-batch fires one push_user_md (debounce=0) and optionally one _enqueue_regen. Returns {results, daily_results, summary}.
dependencies: Postgres (Workout, BodyWeightLog, SleepLog, RestingHeartRateLog, FuelProfile), apps/orchestrator/workspace_envelope.py:push_user_md, QStash (regen_needed→regenerate_fuel_crons task)
inventory_notes: suppress_refresh() + suppress_cron_regen() wrap the ingest loop so 50-item batches don't generate 50 QStash publishes and 50 USER.md pushes. debounce_seconds=0 is load-bearing (shared debounce key with conversation capture — a windowed push would be silently dropped). Auto-complete uses _MIN_DURATION_RATIO=0.5 plausibility gate. Low-signal walks/hikes (raw_type in {walking, hiking}) don't auto-complete non-walk planned sessions. Device timezone is used to self-heal user.timezone if it is still the 'UTC' default.

user_story: As the system, I want to tombstone any deleted HealthKit-anchored workout's external_id so that a HealthKit anchor reset (app reinstall) cannot resurrect deleted workouts.
expected_behaviour: The `workout_deleted_record_healthkit_tombstone` post_delete signal fires on any Workout delete where external_id is non-empty (regardless of source). It appends the external_id to FuelProfile.healthkit_tombstones (FIFO-capped at 200, oldest evicted). The list is consulted by the HealthKit sync ingest loop before processing each item — tombstoned external_ids report status='duplicate' and are not re-created. Errors are swallowed and logged (never block the delete).
inventory_notes: Gated on external_id, not source. A matched planned session (source=user or assistant) that was completed by HealthKit carries the HK sample UUID — deleting it also tombstones the HK sample, preventing re-import.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to log a workout on behalf of a user when they report a session in chat so that training data is recorded from any channel.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/log/ authenticates with X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers. Accepts: category, status (defaults to 'done'), duration_minutes, rpe (1-10), date (resolved via resolve_relative_date in tenant tz; fallback to tenant-local today), activity, detail_json. Runs normalize_detail + validate_detail on detail_json; returns 400 with LLMValidationError envelope on failure. Sets source=ASSISTANT. Calls detect_prs (best-effort). Returns 201 with {id, date, category, activity, status}.
inventory_notes: date resolution uses resolve_relative_date which handles 'today', 'yesterday', ISO strings, and relative phrases. source=ASSISTANT distinguishes chat-logged sessions from HealthKit imports and user-created rows.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to update or delete a specific workout on behalf of a user so that I can correct details or remove accidental entries from chat.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/{workout_id}/ updates fields selectively via update_fields to avoid overwriting HealthKit-synced data. Checks edit lock first (409 + Retry-After if locked). Accepts: activity, category, status, date (ISO), duration_minutes, rpe, notes, detail_json (with normalize+validate). Re-runs detect_prs if detail_json changed. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/{workout_id}/ also checks edit lock. Both return 401 on auth failure, 404 if not found, 409 if edit-locked.
inventory_notes: Uses update_fields=specified+updated_at on save to avoid blind-reverting concurrent HealthKit sync writes. On DELETE, returns {deleted: true, id, activity, date} (not 204).

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to mark a planned workout as skipped with a reason so that adherence is tracked when a user tells me they cannot train.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/{workout_id}/skip/ authenticates with internal headers. Checks edit lock (409 if locked). Sets status=skipped, skip_reason (capped 128 chars). Saves only changed fields. Returns {id, status, skip_reason, date}. 404 if not found, 401 on auth failure.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to mark a planned workout as completed when a user confirms they finished it, and record actual metrics so that training history is accurate.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/{workout_id}/complete/ authenticates with internal headers. Checks edit lock (409 if locked). Sets status=done; optionally updates notes, rpe, duration_minutes. Uses scoped update_fields=['status','notes','rpe','duration_minutes','updated_at'] — does NOT revert HealthKit-synced fields like external_id or merged detail_json. Calls detect_prs (best-effort). Returns {id, status, rpe, duration_minutes, date}.
inventory_notes: Scoped save is deliberate: a full-column save from an in-memory copy would overwrite external_id and merged detail_json that a concurrent HealthKit sync just wrote.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to swap two workouts' scheduled times so that I can reschedule a session at the user's request.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/swap/ authenticates with internal headers. Body: {a: uuid, b: uuid}. Both must belong to the tenant (404). Swaps scheduled_at, window_start_at, window_end_at, date atomically in a DB transaction. Returns {a: {id, scheduled_at, date}, b: {id, scheduled_at, date}}. 400 if a or b missing or identical.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want a comprehensive snapshot of a tenant's recent fitness state so that I can answer questions and give coaching advice with up-to-date context.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/summary/ authenticates with internal headers. Returns: recent_workouts (last 20 done, including distance_km/avg_hr/peak_hr/calories from detail_json), planned_workouts (next 10 planned), active_plans (up to 3 active plans with workout_count/completed_count), latest_body_weight, latest_sleep, latest_resting_hr, trends (weekly_trends dict: sessions_7d/28d, minutes_7d/28d, by_category, recency_days, recent_prs, volume_trend), profile. Returns 401 on auth failure, 404 on tenant not found.
inventory_notes: weekly_trends computes using tenant-local day boundaries. Returns {} for trends if no done workouts in last 28 days. Measured HealthKit metrics (distance_km, avg_hr, peak_hr, calories) are surfaced per-session so the assistant coaches from real data.

feature: Runtime Fuel Audit View (OpenClaw Pre-flight Check)
user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to audit the current workout schedule state before creating or delivering any workout so that I avoid duplicates, contradictions, or orphaned crons.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/audit/ authenticates with internal headers. Cross-references three sources: (1) today's daily-note Fuel section (parsed from Document.kind='daily' via _parse_fuel_section), (2) next 14d Workout rows from Postgres, (3) OpenClaw cron registry (cron.list filtered to _fuel:* and workout-keyword-named crons). Detects conflicts: duplicate_fires (two crons at same schedule), orphan_crons (_fuel:{8-hex} with no matching Workout in next 14d), orphan_workouts (planned with scheduled_at in next 48h but no _fuel: cron). Returns a guidance string instructing the agent to deliver the locked plan verbatim if today_plan exists, or to propose one if not, and to STOP if duplicate fires detected.
dependencies: Postgres (Workout, Document), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.list via invoke_gateway_tool)
inventory_notes: cron.list failure is soft — returns cron_list_error string and empty fuel_crons rather than 500. The _FUEL_SESSION_PREFIX='_fuel:' distinguishes derived per-session crons from legacy plan-named crons. The guidance field is a machine-readable instruction string for the agent.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to log or delete a body weight entry on behalf of a user when they mention their weight in chat.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/body-weight/ authenticates with internal headers. Resolves date via resolve_relative_date in tenant tz (fallback to today_in_tenant_tz). Validates weight_kg (0 < val <= 500). Upserts by (tenant, date). Returns {date, weight_kg, created: bool} with 201/200. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/body-weight/ accepts date via query param or body; returns 404 if no entry for that date, 200 on success.
inventory_notes: Timezone-aware date resolution is critical — see the 2026-05-16 bug where bare date.today() (UTC) caused a JST evening entry to land on yesterday. DELETE accepts date in both query param and request body.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to log a sleep entry when a user tells me how they slept so that recovery data is captured from chat.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/sleep/ authenticates with internal headers. Resolves date via resolve_relative_date in tenant tz. Validates duration_hours (Decimal, 0-24). Optional quality (1-5, clamped) and notes. Upserts by (tenant, date). Returns {date, duration_hours, quality, created: bool} with 201/200. 400 on validation failure.
inventory_notes: No DELETE endpoint for the runtime sleep path (only POST/upsert). Consumer API has PATCH + DELETE.
